name: Avenc de sa Sorpresa
starting_point: Na Burguesa viewpoint in Génova. Follow the path toward Coll des Pastors, then head toward Coll des Vent, detouring at a pass on the slope of Puig d'en Bou
coordinates_utm: DATUM EUROPEAN 1950: 462832 E, 4380171 N. DATUM WGS84: 462730 E, 4379971 N
equipment: Vertical progression equipment and a rope of about 20 m
description: A small but interesting chasm on Puig d'en Bou that likely functions as a natural sinkhole. The entrance (0.90 x 0.50 m) leads to a 5 m descent into a single small chamber with parietal flowstone, beautiful concretions, and a striking column affected by solifluction. Considered a natural site of interest, it can be combined with a visit to a larger nearby cave.
full_description:
Located on Puig d'en Bou is this small but interesting chasm which, although it will certainly not occupy us for an entire day, we can take advantage of a visit to another more substantial cave to then approach it. In my particular case, I visited it after finishing my visit to the Cova d'en Palem.

Located in a small depression in the terrain, this chasm originated from a fracture of the ground, like many others in the area, and may function as a sinkhole for the location. With dimensions of 0.90 x 0.50 m, the entrance is located right at the edge of a small path that departs from the main trail, and therefore finding it will cause us no difficulty even if we go without a GPS.

A tree somewhat removed from the chasm will allow us to set up the rope before beginning the descent into its interior through a shaft of some narrowness that will deposit us at the bottom after a descent of approximately five meters, to then, once "landed," walk through the only small chamber through which we will travel westward along a slight slope whose floor is covered with everything the water drags in from the outside.

As we head toward the back of the chasm, we can observe a wall flowstone that covers the left side of the chasm, although the other side is by no means lacking in concretions — in fact, that side is much more interesting. The deepest part of the cavity is the most ornate, featuring a beautiful column affected by the solifluction process more than half a meter in the vertical direction, not to mention a precious efflorescence that was found in a corner of the chasm.

In summary, a small chasm that is well worth visiting.

The Avenc de sa Sorpresa is considered a natural site of interest. J. A. Encinas S. Inventory list of natural underground spaces of the Balearic Islands. 2006. JAES Editions.
